description: Lock Merchant Arbitration Boards to medieval commercial adjudication forums grounded in merchant/fair courts, contract-law obligations, and guild authentication/enforcement records rather than bill-clearing infrastructure.

checks.reason: The scoped boards are institutional commercial dispute forums tied to merchant, fair, guild, staple, and piepowder courts.
Merchant courts provide the institutional forum rather than a generic historical predecessor.
The boards adjudicated contracts, debts, documents, and mercantile obligations.
Contract law is the shared legal substrate for the disputes without being the board institution itself.
Guild authentication and quality records remain relevant to guild-linked commercial disputes.
Seals and certifications enable authenticated institutional and quality claims for dispute resolution.
Bills of exchange are a possible subject of merchant disputes, not a required institutional prerequisite for arbitration boards.
The node remains anchored to high-medieval merchant/fair court practice at decade precision.
